In the repo at C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow, quickly read these files and return their key relevant content:
1. src/types/report.ts - full file
2. src/report/render/search.ts - just the SearchIndexEntry type and buildSearchIndex function signature
3. src/report/render/markdown.ts - full file (if it exists)
4. src/types/transcript.ts - the ManifestEntry interface

Return the exact TypeScript types/interfaces so I know the current state before writing Phase 2 spec.
I'll help you quickly read those files from the memorytree-workflow skill. Let me start by exploring the structure and reading the files.

Perfect! Here are the key TypeScript types/interfaces from the memorytree-workflow skill:

## 1. src/types/report.ts (Full File)

```typescript
export interface ReportStats {
  totalSessions: number
  totalMessages: number
  totalToolEvents: number
  activeDays: number
  dateRange: { from: string; to: string }
  clientCounts: Record<string, number>
  /** 'YYYY-MM-DD' → session count */
  dayBuckets: Record<string, number>
  /** 'YYYY-WNN' → message count */
  weekBuckets: Record<string, number>
  /** tool name → invocation count */
  toolCounts: Record<string, number>
}

export interface SummaryCache {
  sha256: string
  summary: string
  generated_at: string
}

export interface LinkGraph {
  /** session_id → list of session_ids that reference it */
  backlinks: Record<string, string[]>
  /** session_id → list of session_ids it references */
  forwardLinks: Record<string, string[]>
}

export interface SearchIndexEntry {
  url: string
  title: string
  client: string
  date: string
  snippet: string
}

export interface BuildReportOptions {
  root: string
  output: string
  noAi?: boolean
  model?: string
  /** Build-time locale (e.g. 'en', 'zh-CN'). Default: 'en'. */
  locale?: string
  /** gh-pages branch name. Empty string = skip deploy. Default: ''. */
  ghPagesBranch?: string
  /** Custom domain for CNAME file. Empty string = skip. Default: ''. */
  cname?: string
  /** Webhook URL for post-build notifications. Empty string = skip. Default: ''. */
  webhookUrl?: string
  /** Session IDs newly imported in this heartbeat cycle (for webhook). */
  newSessionIds?: string[]
}
```

## 2. src/report/render/search.ts - SearchIndexEntry and buildSearchIndex

```typescript
export interface SearchIndexEntry {
  url: string
  title: string
  client: string
  date: string
  snippet: string
}

export function buildSearchIndex(
  manifests: ManifestEntry[],
  getSnippet: (m: ManifestEntry) => string,
): SearchIndexEntry[]
```

## 3. src/report/render/markdown.ts (Full File Exists)

Main function is `markdownToHtml(md: string): string` - converts Markdown to HTML with support for headings, bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks, lists, and links. All content is HTML-escaped before structural tags.

## 4. src/types/transcript.ts - ManifestEntry Interface

```typescript
export interface ManifestEntry {
  client: string
  project: string
  session_id: string
  raw_sha256: string
  title: string
  started_at: string
  imported_at: string
  cwd: string
  branch: string
  raw_source_path: string
  raw_upload_permission: string
  global_raw_path: string
  global_clean_path: string
  global_manifest_path: string
  repo_raw_path: string
  repo_clean_path: string
  repo_manifest_path: string
  message_count: number
  tool_event_count: number
  cleaning_mode: string
  repo_mirror_enabled: boolean
}
```

All files are in place and ready for your Phase 2 spec!

'' : '') 23→ inList = false 24→ listType = null 25→ } 26→ } 27→ 28→ while (i < lines.length) { 29→ const line = lines[i] ?? '' 30→ 31→ // Fenced code block 32→ if (line.startsWith('```')) { 33→ closeList() 34→ const lang = line.slice(3).trim() 35→ const codeLines: string[] = [] 36→ i++ 37→ while (i < lines.length && !(lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('```')) { 38→ codeLines.push(lines[i] ?? '') 39→ i++ 40→ } 41→ output.push(`
${escHtml(codeLines.join('\n'))}
`) 42→ i++ 43→ continue 44→ } 45→ 46→ // YAML front matter (skip) 47→ if (i === 0 && line === '---') { 48→ i++ 49→ while (i < lines.length && (lines[i] ?? '') !== '---') i++ 50→ i++ // skip closing --- 51→ continue 52→ } 53→ 54→ // Headings 55→ const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)$/) 56→ if (headingMatch) { 57→ closeList() 58→ const level = headingMatch[1]?.length ?? 1 59→ const text = headingMatch[2] ?? '' 60→ output.push(`${applyInline(text)}`) 61→ i++ 62→ continue 63→ } 64→ 65→ // Horizontal rule 66→ if (/^---+$/.test(line.trim())) { 67→ closeList() 68→ output.push('
') 69→ i++ 70→ continue 71→ } 72→ 73→ // Ordered list 74→ const olMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)\d+\.\s+(.+)$/) 75→ if (olMatch) { 76→ if (!inList || listType !== 'ol') { 77→ closeList() 78→ output.push('
    ') 79→ inList = true 80→ listType = 'ol' 81→ } 82→ output.push(`
  1. ${applyInline(olMatch[2] ?? '')}
    2. `) 83→ i++ 84→ continue 85→ } 86→ 87→ // Unordered list 88→ const ulMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)[-*+]\s+(.+)$/) 89→ if (ulMatch) { 90→ if (!inList || listType !== 'ul') { 91→ closeList() 92→ output.push('
      ') 93→ inList = true 94→ listType = 'ul' 95→ } 96→ output.push(`
    • ${applyInline(ulMatch[2] ?? '')}

      ${applyInline(line)}

      `) 111→ i++ 112→ } 113→ 114→ closeList() 115→ return output.join('\n') 116→} 117→ 118→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 119→// Inline formatting 120→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 121→ 122→function applyInline(text: string): string { 123→ // Process links before HTML-escaping to avoid double-escaping href/label 124→ const withLinks = text.replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, (_, label: string, href: string) => { 125→ return `${escHtml(label)}` 126→ }) 127→ // Escape remaining text, then apply inline patterns on already-safe content 128→ // Split on the tags we just inserted so they are not re-escaped 129→ const parts = withLinks.split(/(]+>.*?<\/a>)/g) 130→ const processed = parts 131→ .map((part, i) => { 132→ if (i % 2 === 1) return part // already-generated tag, pass through 133→ const escaped = escHtml(part) 134→ return escaped 135→ .replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, '$1') 136→ .replace(/\*(.+?)\*/g, '$1') 137→ .replace(/__(.+?)__/g, '$1') 138→ .replace(/_(.+?)_/g, '$1') 139→ .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1') 140→ }) 141→ .join('') 142→ return processed 143→} 144→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Perfect! Here are the key TypeScript types/interfaces from the memorytree-workflow skill:

## 1. src/types/report.ts (Full File)

```typescript
export interface ReportStats {
  totalSessions: number
  totalMessages: number
  totalToolEvents: number
  activeDays: number
  dateRange: { from: string; to: string }
  clientCounts: Record<string, number>
  /** 'YYYY-MM-DD' → session count */
  dayBuckets: Record<string, number>
  /** 'YYYY-WNN' → message count */
  weekBuckets: Record<string, number>
  /** tool name → invocation count */
  toolCounts: Record<string, number>
}

export interface SummaryCache {
  sha256: string
  summary: string
  generated_at: string
}

export interface LinkGraph {
  /** session_id → list of session_ids that reference it */
  backlinks: Record<string, string[]>
  /** session_id → list of session_ids it references */
  forwardLinks: Record<string, string[]>
}

export interface SearchIndexEntry {
  url: string
  title: string
  client: string
  date: string
  snippet: string
}

export interface BuildReportOptions {
  root: string
  output: string
  noAi?: boolean
  model?: string
  /** Build-time locale (e.g. 'en', 'zh-CN'). Default: 'en'. */
  locale?: string
  /** gh-pages branch name. Empty string = skip deploy. Default: ''. */
  ghPagesBranch?: string
  /** Custom domain for CNAME file. Empty string = skip. Default: ''. */
  cname?: string
  /** Webhook URL for post-build notifications. Empty string = skip. Default: ''. */
  webhookUrl?: string
  /** Session IDs newly imported in this heartbeat cycle (for webhook). */
  newSessionIds?: string[]
}
```

## 2. src/report/render/search.ts - SearchIndexEntry and buildSearchIndex

```typescript
export interface SearchIndexEntry {
  url: string
  title: string
  client: string
  date: string
  snippet: string
}

export function buildSearchIndex(
  manifests: ManifestEntry[],
  getSnippet: (m: ManifestEntry) => string,
): SearchIndexEntry[]
```

## 3. src/report/render/markdown.ts (Full File Exists)

Main function is `markdownToHtml(md: string): string` - converts Markdown to HTML with support for headings, bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks, lists, and links. All content is HTML-escaped before structural tags.

## 4. src/types/transcript.ts - ManifestEntry Interface

```typescript
export interface ManifestEntry {
  client: string
  project: string
  session_id: string
  raw_sha256: string
  title: string
  started_at: string
  imported_at: string
  cwd: string
  branch: string
  raw_source_path: string
  raw_upload_permission: string
  global_raw_path: string
  global_clean_path: string
  global_manifest_path: string
  repo_raw_path: string
  repo_clean_path: string
  repo_manifest_path: string
  message_count: number
  tool_event_count: number
  cleaning_mode: string
  repo_mirror_enabled: boolean
}
```

All files are in place and ready for your Phase 2 spec!
